notes.text: User confirmed execution start; prioritize agent-first autonomous meeting with live room visibility and optional human participation
P0-1 draft freeze created: docs/tech/P0-1-mas-runtime-interface-freeze.md; includes SPI/event envelope/room topic/recovery contract
Single primary implementation confirmed: Hybrid MAS only; free-discussion and Debate+Judge remain lightweight evaluation baselines
Per user priority, systematic evaluation moved to P1; P0 focuses on Hybrid implementation and smoke validation
P0 validation clarified: real LLM/agent run required for core path; mock allowed only for infra unit tests
Completed P0-1/P0-2/P0-3 initial code: Hybrid strategies, model router, disaster-only fallback, event contract, tests passing; next is P0-4 real run pending API credentials
Refactored real-run configuration to multi-supplier routing targets with provider registry and namespaced env vars; unit tests updated and passing
Tightened P0 real-run host prompt contract with structured JSON validation and added REAL_RUN_ROUTE override for deterministic default/escalation/fallback checks
Completed cross-role technical workshop on MAS communication/collaboration and drafted ADR-002/ADR-003 to freeze topology, protocol boundary, and phase-1 collaboration mechanism
Synced PRD and execution tech docs with latest architecture decisions, including 4-role phase-1 collaboration, internal room protocol boundary, updated event model, and real-run scope clarification

User requested plan refinement before implementation continues; clarified that the P1 frontend demo must be driven by real room runtime and room event protocol rather than pure mock interaction, and reordered execution to runtime-first before UI acceptance

Front-end stack baseline finalized from official docs: React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + React Router v7 Data Mode + TanStack Query + native WebSocket/SSE; deferred Next.js/SSR-first approach to keep the room runtime boundary independent in P1

Completed the runtime-first implementation scaffold for P1-1/P1-2: added FastAPI room runtime with replay, WebSocket and SSE transport, React/Vite frontend host with room event store and pages for lobby/live room/results; backend unit tests passed, frontend production build passed, and local HTTP room creation/replay verification succeeded

Tightened requirement planner semantics per user direction: room creation now fails fast on planner/upstream errors by default, fallback is explicit-only and reduced to a transparent stub rather than hidden rule-based decomposition, typed planner/provider errors are surfaced through API/UI, and tests/build pass

Refined the next execution phase: P1-3 now starts with replacing DemoAgentExecutor by a real multi-role executor before browser-level MVP validation; local runtime launcher auto-loads .env for development while keeping production env injection as the deployment contract

Completed P1-3a implementation and localhost smoke validation: room runtime now uses a real multi-role executor, recorder schema accepts empty disagreement lists while preserving fail-fast validation, ended rooms reject further human writes with explicit 409 errors, and local verification moved to ports 8010/5174 because an unrelated FastAPI service occupies 8000; remaining runtime risk is explicit upstream timeout on the escalated supplier/model during longer meetings

User requested architecture-first refactor planning before further implementation. Froze MAS harness architecture baseline and execution contracts: top-level layers are Memory / Orchestration / Control / Governance; state is memory projection only; Runtime/Harness is an execution harness rather than a layer; added four refactor sprints to the active plan before continuing broader implementation
Split architecture-first refactor into dedicated child plan PLAN-20260329-002 so the parent plan keeps product-level granularity while the child plan owns A1-A4 architecture refactor sprints

Child plan PLAN-20260329-002 completed A1 Memory Foundation by extracting RoomEventJournal as the single append/replay boundary and adding rebuild evidence from journal replay; next child sprint is A2 Control Consolidation

Child plan PLAN-20260329-002 completed A2 Control Consolidation by moving room-level gating and routing-tier fallback/escalation eligibility into control policies; next child sprint is A3 Orchestration Extraction

Child plan PLAN-20260329-002 completed A3 Orchestration Extraction by moving participant roster and round event sequencing into decision_room/orchestration/room_orchestrator.py; next child sprint is A4 Harness Thinning And Ports/Adapters Cleanup

Child plan PLAN-20260329-002 completed A4 Harness Thinning And Ports/Adapters Cleanup by moving orchestration-owned modules out of runtime/ and leaving runtime as harness plus temporary compatibility shims; umbrella milestone P1-A is complete and focus returns to P1-3b/P1-3c

Completed escalation-path stability diagnosis for P1-3b/P1-3c: direct engineering root cause is now pinned to round-4 fixed escalation plus serial 4-role execution plus glm-4.6 long-tail latency; accepted local stopgap B keeps escalation semantics but points local dev/demo escalation to qwen/qwen-plus only, with evidence, scope, and exit criteria recorded in docs/tech/P1-provider-escalation-diagnostic-20260405.md

Added repeated host/pro/con latency samples and confirmed the escalation diagnosis is stable rather than a one-off measurement; same-layer observation is now sufficient, and the next engineering step should move to remedy-option comparison instead of more blind benchmarking

Superseded the prior P1-3b/P1-3c continuation path after confirming it still depended on fixed rounds, fixed roster, and fixed emission-order assumptions; froze the new host-led meeting-topology baseline and split replan work into child plan PLAN-20260405-003
Created as child execution plan of PLAN-20260301-001 to carry architecture-first refactor sprints A1-A4 under the frozen MAS harness baseline
Completed A1 Memory Foundation: extracted RoomEventJournal, kept projection rebuildable from journal replay, and moved plan focus to A2 Control Consolidation

Started A2 Control Consolidation: introduced RoomControlPolicy for room-level write admission and meeting-end payloads, removed duplicate meeting.ended emission after human override, and kept A2 open pending broader timeout/retry/fallback consolidation

Advanced A2 by moving round budget ownership into RoomControlPolicy/RoomControlConfig and routing runtime loop start, consensus end, budget end, and runtime failure gating through the control outlet; A2 remains open because timeout/retry/fallback eligibility are not yet fully unified
Completed A2 Control Consolidation: routing-tier gating now lives in RoutingControlPolicy, HybridModelRouter only maps tier to target, and the next sprint focus is A3 Orchestration Extraction

Completed A3 Orchestration Extraction: created decision_room/orchestration/room_orchestrator.py, moved participant roster and round event sequencing out of room_runtime.py, and moved plan focus to A4 Harness Thinning And Ports/Adapters Cleanup

Completed A4 Harness Thinning And Ports/Adapters Cleanup: moved brief_planner, room_executor, real_run_contract, and legacy demo_executor out of runtime/ into orchestration/, left compatibility shims only, and closed the child plan

Created as child execution plan of PLAN-20260301-001 after aborting the prior P1 continuation path; new baseline is pre-room planning workflow + Host-led orchestration + dynamic specialists + memory-backed synthesis

Deep diagnosis confirmed a new post-B5 owner drift around room-start contract semantics; follow-on correction moved into child plan PLAN-20260407-004 so the completed mainline replan and the new boundary correction do not share one execution stream
B1-B5 mainline and child corrective plan PLAN-20260407-004 are both implemented and revalidated; parent plan is entering completion confirmation.

Closed the parent P1 host-led execution plan after child plan PLAN-20260407-004 completed the room-start owner correction; B1-B5 mainline and post-B5 correction are now both closed on the same architecture baseline.

Created as child corrective plan of PLAN-20260405-003 after architecture/code/product diagnostics converged on a single new issue: room-start contract semantics owner drift across planner output, preflight rule taxonomy, and harness-injected validation contracts

Plan review corrected the execution contract: C1 must freeze a durable owner split table before code changes, C2 must converge on a single orchestration-owned canonical contract artifact, and C3/C4 now explicitly block relabeled harness semantics from flowing back into core or product surfaces.

Completed C1 by freezing the owner split table in the active plan. C2 now starts from a single target artifact: room_start_contract must replace parallel semantics across raw open_questions, preflight taxonomy, and harness acceptance context.

Completed the corrective execution plan end-to-end: core room-start semantics now converge on the single persisted room_start_contract artifact, frontend product surfaces use a shared allowlisted contract view instead of validation-specific panels, harness usage stays explicit via operator_context, and regression/build validation passed.

Applied a review-driven amendment inside the same plan: canonical room-start assembly no longer depends on the legacy taxonomy path, planning.completed no longer persists parallel room_start_contract_draft or raw planner open_questions artifacts, and home now shares the same product allowlist helper as the other product surfaces.

Created after the user confirmed the next phase should harden evaluation harness governance boundaries before any new product capability work; this plan owns inventory, contract hardening, product-surface separation, and regression closure without reopening the closed room-start owner correction.

Scope-out violation acknowledged: commit 5f47149 shipped a centralized MAS product capability in the same window the plan said 'no new product capability or product IA'. Sprint H3 done signal 'product surfaces continue to expose only allowlisted product-safe contract/read-model sections' was also violated because the new frontend Central MAS panel reads central_mas artifact directly outside PRODUCT_OPERATOR_CONTRACT_SECTIONS. Remediation moved to PLAN-20260513-006 which will replace the stub supervisor with a real LLM-backed supervisor, route central_mas through the product allowlist, and add the H4 negative-case regressions this plan still owes.

Created after architecture review found commit 5f47149 shipped a scripted stance-template stub as the default centralized MAS path with no LLM calls and bypassed the product allowlist. User confirmed direction: replace the stub with a real LLM-driven supervisor that emits assignment contracts, reuse the existing LLMRoomExecutor specialist machinery, route central_mas through the planning-artifacts allowlist, and add H4-style negative regressions previously deferred.

S1-S5 closed in a single session. central_mas.py is now an LLMSupervisor that emits real assignment contracts via one LLM call per round; central_executor.py composes LLMSupervisor with the existing LLMRoomExecutor specialist + synthesis machinery so the centralized topology shares the same provider/routing/retry/parse semantics. CentralizedRequirementPlanner and its answer-shaped _central_constraints helper are deleted. DECISION_ROOM_EXECUTOR default reverted to llm and centralized mode returns UnavailableRoomExecutor when provider env is missing. Frontend reads central_mas through a schema-gated readLatestCentralMasState that drops non-supervisor topologies and unknown fields. Backend 80 tests OK; frontend 9 contract tests OK; production build green.
